name: v1.3.0 local LLM hosting + Models page, compaction engine, audit + soak infra
targetWindow: Planning crystallized 2026-06-17 from the approved brainstorm sketch; work not yet started. Audit-first, dependency-ordered (Phase 0 open -> Phase 3 validate/close). No firm target date set.
updateSource: Crystallized from the parked v1.3.0 brainstorm sketch (/tmp/artifact after v1.2.0 shipped; operator-approved scope 2026-06-17. No theme: chunky-and-complete, selected by priority + readiness.
summary: Feature-tier release (minor, not a patch): promote the v1.2.0 bundled llama.cpp sidecar from embeddings-only to a first-class local LLM host, and stand up a dedicated Models page (model config decomposed out of monolithic Settings, local-host management UI, ModelHub discover/compat/download/SHA-pin/install/activate flow, BYO-GGUF-URL path) that manages embedded AND external local backends as first-class peers (additive: external llama.cpp/Embedded Runtime support is NOT removed). Alongside: an intelligent parallel compaction engine (wheat/chaff off the critical path -> persistence + runtime context reduction), the long-planned execution-grounded real coding benchmark, a post-v1.2.0 wholesale benchmark re-run (now also exercising the local-host route), per-use-case model recommendations turning benchmark scores into a decision (up to 3 local + 3 cloud for coding/general-assistant/chatbot, deterministic over quant/qual/latency, additive and routing-neutral), a fresh cycle-opening triple-lens code audit, soak infrastructure (orchestration harness + gate-on-resolution + the answer-fidelity correct-answer-survival inverse-soak), two contained correctness fixes (per-turn retry-cost ceiling, verifier evidence recovery on TOTOF-collapse), and a full-scale product-knowledge pack refresh (all versions, source-backed, current). Also folds in the SelfHealing: a framework-native, deterministic maintenance/repair subsystem (modeled on RunHygiene, no LLM/no authority negotiation, idempotent) that supersedes the subagent-delegation framing and generalizes self-heal across DATABASE (5 hygiene coverage gaps + 3 recurring sources, with source fixes), WORKSPACE, CONFIGURATION, PLUGINS, SKILLS, and SECURITY (sensitive domains report-and-propose only); per the v1.3.0+ charter the tail domains may extend past v1.3.0.

INSTRUMENTATION HALF DONE 2026-06-19 (commits 6f0d938a, a45dcd92): the leftover direct-stage content_head instrumentation is in place. The assembled-prompt capture now records ContextKind (history vs topic_summary vs memory), and the direct-path guard application records a guard_content_rewrite turn-diagnostics event (content_head before/after + fired guards) whenever the guard chain alters a direct-path answer. This is the DETECTION FOUNDATION the inverse-soak needs: a guard/finalize degrading a CORRECT answer is now provable from recorded state. REMAINING (soak-window): the correct-answer-survival inverse-soak RUNNER, a thin runner over run-agent-behavior-soak.py (same pattern as run-adversarial-soak.py: reuse managed-server + send_message) driven by a CURATED correct-answer-survival scenario set derived from the v1.2.0 audit defects and the false-capability-denial case, asserting a correct model answer survives guard/recovery/finalize. It registers in run-release-soak-suite.py (the suite doc already anticipates it) and must be LIVE-validated in the pre-release soak window (minimize-soak-runs). Writing scenarios blind risks non-reproducing cases (false confidence), so it is built and validated as soak prep, not now.

required.title: Confirm + close out agent tool-use inconsistency (suspected downstream of operator authority)
required.summary: Answer-fidelity/false-incapacity defect (operator 2026-06-17, RCA from prod turn_diagnostic_events: 881 tool_call_blocked, all reason_code=tool_not_selected; the per-turn embedding-pruned surface excludes tools the model legitimately calls, e.g. list_directory). RECONCILED in two stages, both via the design's Expanded() widening valve (widen, not bypass; the surface still SHAPES). STAGE 1 false-capability-denial-guard-suppression (commit 12b46f54): the WORST manifestation (starvation -> false permanent refusal) fixed on the capability-falsehood retry path + grounding denials vs the FULL surface. STAGE 2 GENERAL CASE (code-complete): a plain tool_not_selected block of a REAL registered tool (no capability-falsehood guard firing) on a light+expandable envelope now triggers an Expanded() widening retry instead of leaving the model hard-blocked. Cross-layer wiring: blockToolNotOnSurface records OffSurfaceRealToolBlocked on the shared Session ONLY when l.tools.Get(name)!=nil (real tool; unknown/spurious calls keep the plain block + available-tools enumeration); the guard-retry loop (retryWithGuardsDetailed widenForToolNotSelected hook) reads that one-shot signal and, when no guard requested a retry, widens via Expanded().applyToolPolicy and re-runs so the next surface includes the tool. Reuses the Stage-1 machinery; mutually exclusive with the capability-falsehood widen (guard-fired vs no-guard branches) so no double-widen. Rule-14: turn_envelope_widened_for_tool_not_selected (blocked tools + from/to weight). Full/non-expandable envelopes do NOT widen. RED-first proven at both the agent-loop signal seam and the pipeline retry seam. Status stays in-progress: soak-validated at release per gate-on-resolution (hot-path).
evidence: internal/agent/loop_act_guards.go (blockToolNotOnSurface records real-tool off-surface signal; unknown tools excluded)

planned.title: SelfHealing = framework-native deterministic maintenance subsystem (NOT subagent delegation)
planned.summary: ARCHITECTURE (operator 2026-06-18, SUPERSEDES the 'self-healing orchestrates via subagent delegation' framing). THESIS: fundamental maintenance must be framework-native deterministic the platform, not LLM-driven/authority-gated/refusable delegation. Model: internal/db/hygiene.go RunHygiene on the maintenance heartbeat — generalize to every domain. WHY NOT DELEGATION (explicit non-goal): authority-gated (blocked at peer trust — the false-incapacity authority over-generalization), non-deterministic (can refuse/confabulate/stall), costly (inference on rule-based work), demonstrably failed (agent spawned 125 dead 'workspace-cleanup' pending tasks trying to delegate ONE cleanup it couldn't authorize). The self-healing NEVER routes fundamental upkeep through delegation. ARCH: one subsystem, deterministic per-domain repairers (pure the platform, idempotent, typed sub-report) aggregated into one MaintenanceReport (extends HygieneReport); runs on the maintenance loop AND on-demand (full/single-domain) + report/dry-run mode; runs with SYSTEM authority BY CONSTRUCTION (framework code, not a user-channel action) — DECOUPLES the self-healing from the subagent-spawn-authority defect (that fix is still needed for user-facing delegation but is NO LONGER a prerequisite for maintenance). JUDGMENT ESCALATION (bounded/rare): most maintenance is rule-based + needs no LLM; ONLY genuinely-ambiguous cases (is this file a user's working file or detritus?) may make a NARROW grounding-judge-style call that PROPOSES/quarantines only, never auto-destructive, never a subagent. SAFETY: risk-tiered defaults (low-risk DB/index may auto-apply; WORKSPACE/CONFIG/PLUGINS/SKILLS/SECURITY default report-only/propose until enabled); destructive=quarantine-first/reversible (extend mechanic_quarantine; files move-to-quarantine; DB backup-then-mutate + integrity check; single-writer/quiesced — state.db corrupted once under contention); Rule-14 what/why/reversal; report distinguishes auto-applied vs proposed. ACCEPTANCE: single framework entry point runs all enabled deterministic domain repairers -> one MaintenanceReport (DB+workspace+config+plugins+skills+security), NO dependency on subagent spawning or LLM for rule-based passes; each repairer RED-first + typed counters + quarantine/reversal + report-only default for sensitive domains; any LLM judgment is bounded/propose-only/off the rule-decidable critical path.

planned.title: Embedded Runtime tool_calls arguments serialized as object but Provider A-compat endpoint wants string -> 400 breaks local tool use
planned.summary: BEHAVIORAL-CORRECTNESS / provider-compat (operator-reported 2026-06-18 Windows, code-verified). embeddedRuntimeAdapter.Qualifies(FormatEmbedded) (internal/llm/tool_message_normalization_providers.go:78) serializes assistant tool_calls via embeddedRuntimeAdapter (:189) -> embeddedRuntimeAdapter, emitting function.arguments as a JSON OBJECT (embedded-runtime NATIVE format). But FormatEmbedded is sent to the Provider A-COMPATIBLE endpoint (internal/llm/client_url.go:29 'FormatProviderA, FormatEmbedded, and all others use Provider A-compatible endpoint'), whose schema expects arguments as a STRING. Result: embedded-runtime returns 400 'json: cannot unmarshal object into the platform struct field .messages.tool_calls.function.arguments of type string' on any multi-turn turn that replays a prior tool_call -> breaks tool use for ALL embedded-runtime tool-calling models (local-model-b, local-model-a, etc.) through the framework. Same incoherency class as the config-ceiling triple-schema: two parts of the code disagree on a contract (the message normalizer's wire format vs the endpoint's). Surfaced exercising embedded-runtime/local-model-b DELEGATION:C1. FIX: align FormatEmbedded outbound tool_calls to the endpoint actually used — string arguments (Provider A format, like providerACompatibleToolCalls); if the embedded-runtime-native object-args path is dead (no native endpoint is used), remove it (don't keep a serializer that contradicts the endpoint). RED-first: a FormatEmbedded request replaying a prior tool_call serializes arguments as a STRING.
